Contest SFMTA tow — request hearing & gather evidence
results.description:
## Situation
Paid DMV registration fees Nov 2025 but smog check was never verified, so stickers never sent. Car towed March 2026 for "expired registration." Paid ~$1,000 in tow/impound fees.

## DEADLINE: 30 days from tow date — act NOW
Call Tow Hearing Coordinator: 415-646-2020
Or submit written hearing: [Contest a Tow With a Written Hearing](https://www.sfmta.com/contest-tow-written-hearing)
Schedule page: [Schedule a Tow Hearing](https://www.sfmta.com/getting-around/drive-park/towed-vehicles/contest-tow/schedule-tow-hearing-choose-your-time)

## Steps
1. Get smog check done ASAP — prevents another tow and strengthens your case
2. Complete registration — once smog clears, DMV auto-processes since fees are already paid
3. Request tow hearing within 30 days of tow date (call 415-646-2020)
4. Gather evidence:
   - DMV payment receipt/confirmation from Nov 2025
   - Bank/credit card statement showing the payment
   - Any DMV correspondence showing fees received + smog pending
   - Completed smog certificate (once done)
   - Timeline showing good-faith efforts
5. Submit Proof of Correction for the parking ticket (separate from tow):
   - [Online Proof of Correction Form](https://www.sfmta.com/online-proof-correction-form)
   - Upload signed ticket back + pay $10 fee
6. Apply for first-time tow fee reduction and/or low-income reimbursement at https://sfmta.tfaforms.net/55

## Arguments for the Hearing (in order of strength)

1. PROCEDURAL CHALLENGE (STRONGEST):
CVC 22651(o)(2) REQUIRES the officer to verify DMV records before towing and PROHIBITS towing if they lack immediate access. Request documentation proving the officer ran the DMV check. If they didn't, tow was procedurally invalid.

2. SIX-MONTH THRESHOLD:
CVC 22651(o)(2) only authorizes towing if registration expired MORE than 6 months ago. Count your exact dates — if previous registration expired Oct 2025 or later and tow was early March 2026, you may be UNDER 6 months = tow was unauthorized.

3. GOOD FAITH / PARTIAL COMPLIANCE:
You paid fees in Nov 2025. DMV accepted your money. The holdup was smog verification — a bureaucratic gap, not willful evasion. This shows you were actively trying to comply.

4. "FEES PAID" STATUTORY LANGUAGE:
CVC 4000(a) mentions both "registered" and "appropriate fees have been paid." You satisfied the fee component. Argue meaningful difference between never attempting renewal vs. renewal actively in process.

## Key Contacts
- Tow Hearing Coordinator: 415-646-2020
- SFPD STOP Unit (if SFPD-ordered tow): 415-678-3625
- SFMTA Tow Program: TowProgram@sfmta.com / 415-646-2200
- AutoReturn (impound lot): 415-865-8200
- SFMTA Customer Service: 415-701-3000 (11 S Van Ness, M-F 8am-5pm)
- SF 311: 311 or 415-701-2311

## Legal References
- CVC 22651: [California Code, VEH 22651](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?sectionNum=22651&lawCode=VEH)
- CVC 4000: [California Code, VEH 4000](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=VEH&sectionNum=4000.)
- AB 256 (grace period law): [Bill Text - AB-256 Vehicles: registration.](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billNavClient.xhtml?bill_id=202320240AB256)
- SFMTA Contest a Tow: [Contest a Tow](https://www.sfmta.com/getting-around/drive-park/towed-vehicles/contest-tow)
- SFMTA Reduced Fees: [Waivers for people experiencing homelessness or low-income and reduction for first time tow](https://www.sfmta.com/getting-around/drive-park/towed-vehicles/reduced-fees-first-time-tow-and-low-income-individuals)

## If you win
Full refund of tow + storage fees, mailed 4-6 weeks after hearing.

results.content: Investigate STAY-2612 — Duplicate Upsells Requests Being Created
results.description:
## STAY-2612 — Duplicate Upsells Requests Being Created

**Status:** In Review | **Priority:** Medium | **Assignee:** Agnes Pope
**Hotel:** The Mission Inn Hotel and Spa
**Linear:** [Linear](https://linear.app/canary-technologies/issue/STAY-2612)

---

### The Problem
Guests are ending up with duplicate upsell requests — either showing as multiple entries or inflated quantities when they only intended to submit once.

**Specific examples reported:**
- Melody Kulsic — Valet entry duplicated
- Cynthia Hubach — Parking x3
- Robert Vernon — 2 parking requests x7

---

### Root Cause: STAY-2645 (Missing PMS Code → Silent Failure Loop)

**Status:** Design | **Created by:** Luciano Guasco

When a hotel has PMS integration (e.g. OHIP) but an add-on is missing its PMS code:
1. Guest submits upsell
2. Canary creates the purchase order in DB
3. PMS Gateway pushes to PMS with no code
4. Backend throws 500 error
5. Guest-facing UI shows "purchase failed"
6. Guest retries → another purchase order created → duplicates pile up

Multiple purchase orders then display in the add-on Past Items view.

**Proposed solutions (from STAY-2645):**

Option A — Still create the PurchaseOrder:
- Filter out add-ons without PMS code at creation time
- Don't throw 500 back to the UI
- Confirm PurchaseOrder created, mark as PMSErrored
- Show PMSErrored status in the Purchase Orders dashboard

Option B — Don't create PurchaseOrder when PMS errors:
- If no PMS Code, disable the add-on (configurable per hotel)
- Change flow: try PMS first, only create PurchaseOrder on success
- Log error more precisely in Datadog
- Show purchase failed in frontend

**Design decision needed:** How should the admin UI indicate add-ons missing PMS codes? And should errored purchases be visible in the dashboard?

---

### Related: STAY-2502 — Higher Than Normal Upsells Volume

**Hotel:** Papago Scottsdale by Reside, Trademark Collection by Wyndham
**Status:** Done | **Assignee:** Marco Sevilla

Wyndham support called in reporting instances of higher-than-normal upsells volume. Couldn't tell if it was guest behavior, an integration bug, or something else. Likely the same duplication bug manifesting as inflated numbers. Resolved.

---

### Connected Design Tickets
- **DSN-1684** — Visually indicate when Upsells are missing POS Code (Backlog)
- **STAY-2645** — In "Design" status, needs UX decision on error handling
- **DSN-1754** — Rethink UX for cart building to avoid wrong quantity submissions (Todo, High)

---

### Broader Pattern
This ticket is part of the upsell quantity confusion cluster — the #1 customer research opportunity identified in the Mar 26 analysis. Guldsmeden ($80K ARR, 7 hotels) has been reporting related issues for 1.5+ years. The root cause spans both technical (PMS code validation) and UX (guest mental model for quantities) problems.

results.content: Cameron Pherson interview — review case study + prep panel questions

**Context:** You're on the interview panel (not being interviewed). 2nd Round Virtual Interview — Cameron Pherson for Senior Product Manager, Enterprise.

**Prep:**
- Review the case study (check email for materials)
- Prep 3-5 good questions that test PM thinking: prioritization, stakeholder management, data-informed decisions, enterprise complexity

**Don't overprep** — you know product. Just review the case study and jot down questions.

results.content: Design Twitter — draft first 2-3 posts
results.description:
**Timebox: 10 min**

**Goal:** Break the ice. Your first posts don't need to be perfect — they need to exist.

**Quick ideas:**
- Share a design insight from your Canary work (anonymized)
- Hot take on a design trend
- Share the typography comparison tool as a side project showcase
- "Day in the life" of a product designer at a hospitality tech startup

**Rule:** Draft them, post at least one. Don't iterate endlessly. Ship.
